meaning: n.羽毛初长的雏鸟,刚会飞的幼鸟,无经验的人,初出茅庐的人
examples: I've known him ever since he was a fledgling.
One positive outcome that could arise from bumping into a friendly ETI is that it takes our fledgling race under its wing (tentacle).
His conquest of Silesia gave Prussia's fledgling industries access to raw materials.
Several years ago, Bruce Judson experimented with online advertising to attract prospects to his fledgling businesses.
It was about a fledgling charity formed by author and Detroit media personality Mitch Albom.
